Landing Page.behavioral_description: Hero-driven landing page with a bold above-the-fold section. Large headline with subtext and primary CTA button. Below-fold sections present features in alternating layout (text-left/image-right, then reversed). Social proof section with logos or testimonials. Final CTA section before footer. Hero CTA has a hover scale effect. Scroll-triggered fade-in animations for below-fold sections (staggered by 100ms per element). Sticky nav appears after scrolling past hero. Mobile: hamburger menu with slide-in drawer.
Landing Page.accessibility_notes: Hero heading must be h1, sections use h2. Skip-to-content link at top. Auto-rotating carousels must have pause control and aria-live='polite'. CTA buttons need descriptive text (not just 'Click here'). Decorative images use aria-hidden='true', meaningful images need alt text. Color contrast: all text meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 normal, 3:1 large). Reduced motion: respect prefers-reduced-motion for animations.

Dashboard.behavioral_description: Data-dense overview screen. Primary KPI card spans 2 columns with large value (text-[2.5rem] tracking-[-0.03em] tabular-nums). 2-4 secondary KPIs in remaining grid columns at text-3xl. Below KPIs: main chart area (col-span-2) beside a secondary panel (col-span-1) — NEVER equal width. Data table below with sticky header, sort indicators, and text-[13px] body. Skeleton loading matches exact card dimensions with pulse animation. Date range picker in top-right header area. Sidebar nav is quiet (w-[240px], muted text, no colored backgrounds). Zero entrance animations — data appears instantly. All numbers use tabular-nums.
Dashboard.accessibility_notes: ARIA landmarks for nav, main, complementary. Data table has proper th scope. All interactive elements have visible focus indicators. Color contrast meets WCAG AA. Sort buttons have aria-sort. Date picker is keyboard accessible. Stat cards use aria-label for screen readers with value + trend.

Analytics.behavioral_description: Chart-forward layout with 3-6 KPI cards above main chart area. Primary metric gets visual emphasis (larger text or 2-column span). Charts use muted colors — primary line in near-white, secondary in zinc-600 dashed. Grid lines barely visible (opacity 0.04). Legend inline in chart card header, not below. Axis labels in text-[10px] muted text. Date range picker controls all charts. Data table below with export option. Tooltip is solid dark background, not glass. No 3D chart effects.
Analytics.accessibility_notes: Charts have text alternatives (aria-label or data table). Data table sortable by keyboard. Date picker accessible. Color is not sole indicator of trends — use arrows and text alongside color.

Portfolio.behavioral_description: Full-bleed portfolio with large project thumbnails. Minimal chrome — the work speaks. Image-first layout with project title and category appearing on hover (opacity transition, 200ms). Filter by category/tag with animated layout shift. Lazy-loaded images with blur-up placeholder. Optional detail page with full case study: brief, challenge, solution, results. Masonry or grid layout (not carousel). About section with bio and contact CTA at bottom.
Portfolio.accessibility_notes: Project images need descriptive alt text. Hover overlays must be accessible by keyboard (focus state mirrors hover). Filter buttons use aria-pressed for active state. Smooth scroll respects prefers-reduced-motion. Contact form follows standard accessible form patterns.

E-commerce.behavioral_description: Product grid loads with skeleton cards. Filters update results without page reload (faceted search sidebar or horizontal filter bar). Add-to-cart shows micro-animation (subtle icon bounce, count update in header). Product images support zoom on hover, swipe gallery on mobile. Price displays with strike-through for discounts. Out-of-stock items show 'Notify me' instead of add-to-cart. Cart persists across sessions via localStorage. Quick-view modal shows key details without leaving listing.
E-commerce.accessibility_notes: Product cards need descriptive labels: 'Product Name, $Price, Rating'. Image galleries need keyboard navigation (left/right arrows). Add-to-cart button announces success via aria-live region. Filters use fieldset/legend grouping. Price ranges use aria-valuemin/max. Sort dropdown uses native select or combobox pattern. Focus management: after adding to cart, focus stays on product.

Corporate.behavioral_description: Professional layout with clear information hierarchy. Large hero with brand imagery and concise mission statement. Team section with headshots in a grid — not cards, just images with name/role below. Partners section with muted logo grid (grayscale logos, subtle hover colorization). Contact section with minimal form (3-4 fields max) and office locations. Careers link prominent in nav. Press/news section with card list. Typography-driven design, not image-heavy.
Corporate.accessibility_notes: Team member images need name alt text. Map embed needs aria-label and keyboard fallback. Contact form follows accessible form patterns. Logo images need company name alt text.

SaaS.behavioral_description: Product marketing page focused on feature demonstration. Hero with product screenshot or demo video (not stock photos). Feature sections alternate between text and product UI screenshots showing the actual feature. Social proof with logos AND specific metrics ('Used by 10,000+ teams'). Pricing section with 3 tiers, popular tier highlighted. Bottom CTA section with final conversion push. Sticky nav with 'Get Started' button always visible.
SaaS.accessibility_notes: Hero heading must be h1. Product screenshots need alt text describing the feature shown. Video must have captions. Pricing comparison table needs proper headers. CTA buttons are descriptive. Skip navigation link provided.

Documentation.behavioral_description: Three-column layout: left sidebar (nav tree), center (content), right sidebar (on-page TOC). Content column max-width 680px for readability. Code blocks with syntax highlighting and copy button. Inline code uses monospace with subtle background. Headings are anchor-linked. Search opens a command palette (cmd+K). Tab groups for language/framework switching. Callout boxes for warnings/tips (not colored backgrounds — use left border accent).
Documentation.accessibility_notes: Navigation tree uses proper ARIA tree role. Code blocks are announced as code. Search dialog uses combobox pattern. Tab panels properly linked. Heading anchors don't break document outline.

Chat Interface.behavioral_description: Two-panel layout: conversation list (left, w-[280px]) and active chat (right, flex-1). Messages stack bottom-up with newest at bottom. Auto-scroll to newest message. Own messages right-aligned with accent background, others left-aligned with surface background. Typing indicator (3 animated dots) appears inline in message area. Message input fixed at bottom with send button. New messages appear with subtle opacity fade (100ms). Message status (sent, delivered, read) shown with small check icons below message.
Chat Interface.accessibility_notes: Messages use aria-live for new incoming. Conversation list is navigable by keyboard. Input has proper label. Emoji picker accessible. Message timestamps accessible but visually subtle.

Agency.behavioral_description: Portfolio-driven agency site. Full-bleed hero with reel or project showcase. Case study grid below with large images and minimal text overlay. About section with team photos in a horizontal scroll or grid. Services listed as concise cards, not paragraphs. Testimonial from a specific named client with their photo. Contact section with calendar booking link, not a generic form. Minimal pages: Work, About, Contact.
Agency.accessibility_notes: Video hero must have pause control. Case study images need descriptive alt text. Horizontal scroll sections need keyboard navigation. Contact booking iframe needs accessible label.

Admin Panel.behavioral_description: Sidebar navigation (w-[240px]) with collapsible sections for resource types (Users, Orders, Content, Settings). Main area shows data table by default with search, filter, and bulk action toolbar. Detail view uses a side panel or full-page form. CRUD forms use consistent field layouts: label above input, helper text below, validation on blur. Toast notifications for success/error. Breadcrumbs for navigation context. Role-based visibility on nav items.
Admin Panel.accessibility_notes: ARIA landmarks for sidebar, main content. Data tables have proper th scope and sort indicators. Forms follow label-input-error pattern. Toast announced via aria-live. Breadcrumbs use nav with aria-label='Breadcrumb'.

Media Player.behavioral_description: Full-width player bar fixed at bottom (h-[72px]) with: play/pause, progress bar (custom range input), volume, current/total time, track info (thumbnail, title, artist). Queue view in a slide-up panel. Playlist in left sidebar or overlay. Album art dominant in now-playing view. Dark interface by default. Progress bar shows buffer state in lighter shade. Keyboard shortcuts: space=play/pause, arrows=seek, m=mute.
Media Player.accessibility_notes: Custom controls must be keyboard accessible. Progress bar uses role=slider with aria-valuenow/min/max. Play state announced via aria-live. Track info has proper heading hierarchy. Volume slider accessible.

Pricing.behavioral_description: Three pricing tiers in cards with highlighted 'popular' option using accent border or subtle background shift (NOT a badge). Toggle between monthly/annual billing — annual shows savings. Feature comparison in a clean table below cards (not inside cards). CTA buttons say specific actions ('Start free trial', 'Contact sales'), not generic 'Buy'. Enterprise tier links to contact form. Price updates are animated on toggle (number transition). FAQ section below.
Pricing.accessibility_notes: Toggle has proper role and aria-checked. Feature comparison table has proper headers. Price changes announced to screen readers. FAQ uses disclosure pattern (details/summary or aria-expanded).

Calendar.behavioral_description: Multiple views: day, week, month accessible via tab group. Events shown as colored blocks with duration-based height. Click empty space to create event. Drag to resize/move events. Event popover on click shows details with edit/delete. Color coding by calendar source, not event type. Mini calendar in sidebar for quick navigation. Current time indicator (thin red line) in day/week view.
Calendar.accessibility_notes: Calendar grid uses proper table role with date headers. Events have aria-label with full details (title, time, calendar). Keyboard navigation between days/events using arrow keys. Screen reader announces date on focus.

Social Feed.behavioral_description: Single-column feed (max-w-[600px] centered) with post cards. Each post: avatar + name + timestamp header, content area, interaction bar (like, comment, share counts). New posts appear at top with subtle slide-in. Infinite scroll for pagination. Image posts show grid layout (1-4 images). Link previews with thumbnail, title, domain. Reply threads indent slightly. Pull-to-refresh on mobile.
Social Feed.accessibility_notes: Posts are article elements. Like/share buttons have aria-label with count. Image grids have descriptive alt text. Timestamp uses time element with datetime attribute. Infinite scroll has 'Load more' fallback button.

Checkout.behavioral_description: Two-column layout: form (left, 60%) and order summary (right, 40%). Multi-step: shipping → payment → review. Progress indicator at top. Form fields: minimal required, smart defaults, inline validation on blur. Order summary is sticky on scroll. Payment section shows card icons, integrates Stripe Elements. Review step shows editable summary. 'Place Order' button disabled until valid, shows loading state on click.
Checkout.accessibility_notes: Form follows label-input-error pattern strictly. Progress indicator uses aria-current='step'. Card number input has aria-label. Order summary is in an aside with aria-label. Error messages linked to fields via aria-describedby.

Settings.behavioral_description: Vertical tab navigation (left sidebar, w-[200px]) with setting groups: Profile, Account, Billing, Notifications, Integrations. Main area shows form fields for selected section. Toggle switches for boolean settings. Destructive actions (delete account) at bottom with red text and confirmation modal. Auto-save with subtle 'Saved' toast, or explicit Save button at section bottom. Avatar upload with crop modal.
Settings.accessibility_notes: Tab navigation uses proper ARIA tab pattern. Toggle switches have role=switch. Destructive actions need confirmation. Auto-save announced via aria-live. File upload accessible.

CRM.behavioral_description: Kanban or table view toggle for contacts/deals pipeline. Kanban columns represent deal stages with drag-and-drop cards. Table view with inline editing for key fields. Contact detail opens in a slide-over panel (not a new page). Activity timeline in detail view showing calls, emails, notes chronologically. Quick actions: log call, send email, add note. Search with recent contacts. Pipeline value totals shown per column header.
CRM.accessibility_notes: Kanban has keyboard drag-and-drop alternative. Table supports inline editing with Enter/Escape. Slide-over panel traps focus. Activity timeline uses ordered list. Quick actions have descriptive labels.

Onboarding.behavioral_description: 3-step progressive onboarding: 1) Use case selection with radio cards showing icons and descriptions, 2) Team/workspace setup (name, invite), 3) Template or starter selection with live previews. Progress indicator shows current step with completed checkmarks. Skip option always visible but not prominent. Each step validates before advancing. Final step shows the actual product with their data seeded.
Onboarding.accessibility_notes: Step changes announced via aria-live. Radio cards have proper role. Progress bar has aria-valuenow. Focus management: first field focused on each step transition. Skip button always keyboard accessible.

Kanban Board.behavioral_description: Columns represent statuses (To Do, In Progress, Review, Done). Cards show: title (font-medium), assignee avatar (w-5 h-5), priority dot (w-1.5 h-1.5 colored), and optional due date in muted text. Drag and drop between columns with insertion indicator line. Column header shows title + count badge. 'Add card' button at column bottom (ghost style). Click card opens detail modal with description, comments, attachments.
Kanban Board.accessibility_notes: Drag and drop has keyboard alternative (select card, arrow keys to move). Card details accessible via Enter key. Column counts announced with column name. Priority indicated by both colored dot and text label.

Profile.behavioral_description: Cover image at top (h-[200px]) with overlapping avatar (w-20 h-20, -mt-10 relative to cover). Name and bio below avatar, left-aligned. Stats row: followers, following, posts as inline text with numbers in font-semibold. Action buttons: Follow (primary), Message (secondary). Tabbed content below: Posts feed, About info, Media gallery. Edit profile button (ghost style) for own profile.
Profile.accessibility_notes: Avatar has name in alt text. Stats are semantically marked up. Tab panels properly linked to tab buttons with aria-controls/aria-labelledby. Edit profile accessible.

Marketplace.behavioral_description: Filter sidebar (w-[260px]) with category checkboxes, price range slider, rating filter stars. Product grid (3-4 columns) shows: image, title (font-medium text-[14px]), price, rating (stars + count). Sort dropdown in toolbar above grid. Lazy-loaded images with aspect-ratio-square. Product cards have hover overlay showing quick-view button. Mobile: filters in a slide-out drawer.
Marketplace.accessibility_notes: Filter changes announced via aria-live. Product images have descriptive alt text. Price and discounts clearly labeled. Grid layout announced to screen readers. Star rating has text alternative.

File Explorer.behavioral_description: Two-panel: folder tree sidebar (w-[240px]) and file listing main area. Toggle between grid (thumbnails with name below) and list (icon + name + size + modified date columns) view. Breadcrumb navigation above file listing. Drag and drop for moving files. Right-click context menu for rename, delete, move, copy. Multi-select with Shift/Ctrl+Click for bulk operations toolbar. New folder/upload buttons in toolbar.
File Explorer.accessibility_notes: File list uses proper grid role. Context menu accessible via keyboard (Shift+F10 or Menu key). Drag and drop has cut/paste keyboard alternative. File type indicated by both icon and text label. Breadcrumbs use nav with aria-label.

Blog Post.behavioral_description: Long-form content with a narrow reading column (max-w-[680px] mx-auto). Article header with title (text-3xl font-bold tracking-tight), author, date, and reading time. Body uses text-[16px] leading-[1.75] for readability — this is one of the FEW places 16px body is correct. Code blocks with syntax highlighting. Images full-bleed or contained with captions. Table of contents sidebar on desktop. Share and bookmark buttons.
Blog Post.accessibility_notes: Article uses semantic article element. Headings create proper document outline (h1 title, h2 sections, h3 subsections). Images have descriptive alt text. Code blocks marked as code. Reading time is informational, not blocking.

Login.behavioral_description: Centered card (max-w-[400px]) on minimal background. Logo at top of card. Email input with label above. Password input with show/hide toggle. 'Sign in' primary button full-width. 'Forgot password?' link below button. Divider with 'or continue with' text. OAuth buttons (Google, GitHub) as secondary bordered buttons. 'Don't have an account? Sign up' at bottom. No hero images, no sidebars — pure utility.
Login.accessibility_notes: Form uses proper label-input association. Password toggle has aria-label. OAuth buttons have full text labels, not just icons. Error messages linked via aria-describedby. Auto-focus on email input.

Signup.behavioral_description: Similar to Login but with additional fields: name, email, password (with strength indicator). Optional: two-column layout with value proposition on left, form on right. Password requirements shown below field, checking off as met. Terms checkbox before submit. 'Already have an account? Sign in' at bottom. Progressive: can split into 2 steps (account → profile) for complex onboarding.
Signup.accessibility_notes: Password strength indicator has aria-live and text description, not just color. Terms checkbox properly labeled with link to terms. Field validation on blur with aria-describedby errors.

Other.behavioral_description: Clean single-column or two-column layout appropriate to content type. Consistent with the rest of the application's design system. Uses standard component patterns (cards, lists, forms) without introducing novel interaction patterns. Navigation matches the application shell. Loading and error states follow the same patterns as other pages.
Other.accessibility_notes: Follows all standard WCAG AA guidelines. Consistent heading hierarchy. Proper landmarks. Focus management for dynamic content. Color not used as sole indicator.
